div.h1: One-Shot vs Repeating Timer: Heap State Before and After Expiry

g.text: AFTER (cb did NOT cancel): Timer Rescheduled

div.p: ⚠ CRITICAL ORDERING INVARIANT CORRECT (implemented above): pool[slot].expiry_ms = prev + interval ← RESCHEDULE FIRST sift_down(r, 0) cb(tid, udata) ← cb fires SECOND if cb calls cancel → finds live entry → removes correctly WRONG (if order reversed): cb(tid, udata) ← fires first if cb calls cancel → removes entry (heap shrinks) pool[slot].expiry_ms = prev + interval ← RESCHEDULE NOW ADDS GHOST sift_up / sift_down on already-cancelled slot → ghost entry with timer_id=99; next expire closes random conn
heap[0] → pool[slot] timer_id = 99 expiry_ms = 5000 ≤ now_ms() = 35000 interval_ms = 5000 ← repeating heap_idx = 0 cb = on_heartbeat udata = server*
① Capture before mutation tid ← pool[slot].timer_id = 99 interval ← pool[slot].interval_ms = 5000 cb ← pool[slot].callback udata ← pool[slot].udata
② interval_ms > 0 → RESCHEDULE pool[slot].expiry_ms = 5000 + 5000 = 40000 sift_down(r, 0) ← DO NOT call heap_cancel_at_slot ← DO NOT memset the slot timer_heap_size unchanged = M

③ Call cb(tid=99, udata) on_heartbeat fires cb may call: reactor_cancel_timer(r, 99) → scan finds pool[slot].timer_id==99 pool[slot].active==1 ← still there! → heap_cancel_at_slot removes it → returns 0 (success) OR cb returns without cancelling → timer fires again at expiry=40000
pool[slot] — RESCHEDULED timer_id = 99 (unchanged) expiry_ms = 40000 ← next fire interval_ms = 5000 active = 1 (still live) heap position may differ (sift_down moved it)
pool[slot] — CANCELLED during cb reactor_cancel_timer(99) found it heap_cancel_at_slot executed memset → zeros timer_heap_size = M-1
